news.title: Access Determines Price: The Global Crab Market Tightens in 2026
news.excerpt: Global crab trade expanded in 2025, but 2026 is developing into a more difficult operating environment defined by tighter positioning and more contested negotiations. The structure of the market continues to narrow.

news.title: US Extends Russian Seafood Ban as Alaska Pushes for Continued Restrictions
news.excerpt: The US has renewed its national emergency declaration targeting Russian activities, effectively extending the ban on Russian seafood imports that has benefited Alaska's fishing industry since 2022.

news.title: Gulf Snow Crab Season Gets Underway as Newfoundland Harvesters Weigh Price Decision
news.excerpt: Snow crab fishermen in the Gulf of St. Lawrence have begun the 2026 season after brief ice-related delays, moving quickly to capitalize on fishing opportunities.

news.title: ANALYSIS: Argentina's Illex Squid Landings Surge 73% Year to Date, While Prices Show Upward Pressure
news.excerpt: The Illex squid season has been under operation since February 2, 2026. According to Argentina's Ministry of Agriculture, cumulative landings from January 1 to March 31 totaled 169,387 MT, up 73% from 97,829 MT in 2025.

news.title: NOAA Enforcement Nets New Jersey Captain, Seafood Dealer in Illegal Scallop Scheme
news.excerpt: NOAA Fisheries' Office of Law Enforcement highlighted its successful prosecution of a New Jersey fishing captain and seafood dealer who operated an illegal scallop harvesting conspiracy.

news.title: ANALYSIS: February Dip in French Shrimp Imports Reflects Typical Seasonal Slowdown
news.excerpt: The French National Directorate of Foreign Trade Statistics published the latest figures for French imports last week, showing a 22.0% decline in February versus January.

news.title: Trident Seafoods Files $100 Million Suit Alleging Tacoma Fire Department Destroyed Kodiak Enterprise
news.excerpt: More than three years after a devastating fire aboard a commercial fishing vessel, Trident Seafoods has filed a lawsuit against the City of Tacoma seeking $100 million in compensation.

news.title: UK Fish Stocks Face Growing Strain as Sustainability Ratings Slip for Cod, Scampi
news.excerpt: The Marine Conservation Society has issued a fresh warning about mounting pressure on UK fish stocks, with revised ratings showing cod and langoustine are becoming less sustainable.

news.title: PEIXE BR Welcomes Brazil's New Fisheries Minister Araújo
news.excerpt: The Brazilian Association of Fish Farming welcomed the appointment of Édipo Araújo as Minister of Fisheries and Aquaculture in an April 13 announcement.

news.title: ASC-Led Partnership Launches Sustainable Aquaculture Initiative in Songkhla Lake
news.excerpt: A new partnership led by the Aquaculture Stewardship Council has launched a sustainable aquaculture initiative in Thailand's Songkhla Lake.

news.title: Cousins Maine Lobster Rolls Into Savannah and St. Louis with New Food Trucks
news.excerpt: Cousins Maine Lobster is expanding its food truck fleet with new locations in Savannah, Georgia and St. Louis, Missouri.

news.title: Albertsons Reports Fiscal 2025 Results and Issues Fiscal 2026 Guidance
news.excerpt: Albertsons Companies has reported its fiscal 2025 results and provided guidance for fiscal 2026, including updates on its seafood sales performance.
